author: Early Corpus Analyst Cedar

dimensionSummaries.dimension.player-fantasy: The player becomes a traveling swordsman whose bodily skill, learned techniques, magic, and numeric growth all visibly improve across a hazardous kingdom.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.core-loop: The cadence alternates strategic overworld travel with side-view action spaces, then converts combat experience, town assistance, and palace completion into stronger future attempts.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.verbs: Side-view movement supports jumping, high and low sword attacks, shield positioning, learned thrusts, and selectable spells, while the overworld abstracts travel and encounters.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.world-state: Palace crystals, learned spells and techniques, key items, opened routes, and character levels persist, while roaming encounter icons create temporary local threats.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.topology: A top-down regional map connects towns, caves, bridges, palaces, and terrain-triggered action scenes; side-view spaces then express the local path and combat geometry.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.gating-revisit: Spells, quest items, movement techniques, and palace tools unlock terrain or interactions, repeatedly sending the player from regional routes back to towns and blocked passages.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.puzzle-information: Towns and caves distribute short clues, while local side-view layouts ask players to connect those statements with item use, magic, hidden passages, and traversal timing.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.dungeon-structure: Palaces are branching side-view room networks with elevators, locked doors, keys, a major item, a guardian, and a crystal-placement objective.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.exploration-reward: Optional caves, town services, hidden tiles, and combat risks can yield experience, magic or life growth, containers, techniques, or information needed elsewhere.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.growth-economy: Experience points can be assigned at thresholds to attack, magic, or life levels, creating a visible tradeoff between immediate improvement and saving toward a costlier attribute.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.combat-boss: Combat emphasizes spacing, jump arcs, shield height, enemy guard position, magic expenditure, and precise sword contact within constrained side-view lanes.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.npc-quest: Town residents give clues and services, and several teachers or spell-granting characters respond only after the player resolves a local need or finds a required person or object.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.narrative-performance: The sleeping-princess premise frames a pilgrimage, while towns, palace crystal placements, and the final temple turn progression into repeated acts of restoration and qualification.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.camera-blocking: The overworld camera frames regional choice, then contact or entry cuts to side-view scenes where platforms, enemies, and shield lines are staged laterally.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.teaching-feedback: Early action scenes expose jumping, sword height, and shield behavior directly; level-up prompts, spell effects, and NPC responses make new rules immediately testable.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.ui-input: A status interface tracks life, magic, experience, and three levels; pause selection assigns a spell while directional and button combinations control side-view technique.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.recovery-accessibility: Extra lives absorb some failures, but losing the active stock returns the player to the starting palace and can erase unbanked experience, making route preparation consequential.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.perspective-implementation: The mixed perspective separates high-level navigation from detailed platform combat, requiring explicit transitions and different collision, movement, and encounter rules for each mode.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The original Disk System game is single-player and maps its mixed-mode action, menu selection, and save data to a compact digital controller.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.production-runtime: Its principles require two movement models, encounter spawning on a regional map, side-view combat physics, attribute thresholds, magic costs, lives, and persistent quest and palace flags.

observationThemes.context: While Link travels as a small figure on the regional map, roaming enemy markers can approach and make contact.
preconditions: The player is traveling outside a town or palace
A roaming encounter marker is active nearby
playerInput: Either evade the marker or allow contact and then traverse the side-view encounter
before: Travel is represented at regional scale
No local combat arena is active
action: Contact an enemy marker and fight or traverse the resulting action scene
after: The player returns to the regional map if the scene is exited
Defeated enemies may have added experience toward an attribute threshold
feedback: The view switches from top-down map travel to a side-view lane
Enemy defeat and the experience display report incremental growth
consequences: Travel itself becomes a risk-management decision
Repeated mastery can be converted into attack, magic, or life improvement
observationThemes.factualDescription: Contact with a roaming marker on the top-down map opens a side-view action encounter; completing or leaving that space returns Link to regional travel and combat can add experience.
observationThemes.interpretation: Expanding a map contact into embodied combat lets one journey alternate planning and execution while tying the player's growing dexterity to an explicit long-term growth economy.

observationThemes.context: Regional travel exposes terrain and side-view passages that cannot be crossed until the player has learned a spell, technique, or obtained a key item.
preconditions: A blocked route has been observed
The required persistent capability is later acquired through exploration or town assistance
playerInput: Invoke the learned capability at the compatible obstruction
before: The route graph ends at an obstacle
The next region or reward cannot be reached
action: Use the relevant spell, movement technique, or item in the blocked context
after: The obstacle becomes passable or its hidden route becomes legible
The regional graph gains a new reachable branch
feedback: The avatar's action or spell visibly changes the traversal condition
Crossing the former boundary confirms the capability match
consequences: The player reaches a palace, town, container, or further clue
Earlier geography is reinterpreted as a list of capability-dependent returns
observationThemes.factualDescription: Persistent spells, learned sword techniques, and quest items are used at compatible overworld or side-view obstacles to make previously blocked routes traversable.
observationThemes.interpretation: When instruction and capability acquisition occur away from the obstacle, successful return joins social memory, regional navigation, and mechanical growth into one payoff.

observationThemes.context: A palace branches through locked rooms, elevators, enemies, and hazards before yielding a major item and placing a guardian near its completion objective.
preconditions: The player has enough keys, health, and combat ability to explore its branches
playerInput: Read side-view geometry and enemy guards
Acquire the palace item and apply learned combat or traversal rules to reach the guardian
before: Parts of the palace and later world remain inaccessible
The palace crystal objective is unfinished
action: Navigate the dependency chain, defeat the guardian, and place a crystal
after: The major item remains available outside the palace
feedback: Doors, elevators, item presentation, guardian defeat, and crystal placement mark each dependency
Side-view staging exposes attack height and contact results
consequences: A campaign-level qualification step is completed
The acquired tool can change later routes and encounters
observationThemes.factualDescription: Each palace combines branching side-view navigation, locks, enemies, a persistent item, a guardian battle, and a crystal-placement completion state.
observationThemes.interpretation: A bounded challenge can make a tool acquisition serve two scales at once: it resolves local dependencies and becomes evidence that the player is ready for harder regional traversal.

observationThemes.context: In a town, residents occupy lateral streets and house interiors; a teacher or spell-granting character may withhold help until a related local need is resolved.
preconditions: The player lacks the offered spell or technique
playerInput: Find the requested person or object
Return to the character who can teach
before: A resident communicates a need or condition
The capability is absent from the player's status and input vocabulary
action: Complete the local request and re-enter the teacher's interaction
after: The spell or technique is learned persistently
Its selection or button combination becomes usable
feedback: The side-view street and house transitions stage the search as a short social route
Text and a visible status or action change confirm the lesson
consequences: A new combat or traversal solution becomes available
Town narrative directly changes the player's mechanical repertoire
observationThemes.factualDescription: Town conversations can establish a local condition whose completion causes another resident to grant a persistent spell or sword technique with a usable control expression.
observationThemes.interpretation: Turning help into embodied instruction makes an NPC exchange mechanically consequential and lets a small town function as both narrative scene and capability hub.

observationThemes.context: A single-player attempt carries a finite life stock and a current experience total through transitions between regional travel and side-view hazards.
preconditions: The player has not converted all current experience into levels
Repeated health depletion consumes the remaining life stock
playerInput: If the stock is exhausted, resume from the starting palace and rebuild the route
before: The player is deep in a route with accumulated but unbanked experience
The active life stock still represents retry capacity
action: Lose all health and eventually exhaust the current lives
after: The player returns to the campaign's starting recovery point
Persistent levels and key progress remain while unbanked experience is at risk
feedback: Life loss, game-over flow, status totals, and the familiar starting room make the recovery transaction explicit
The mixed-mode runtime reloads a stable campaign state
consequences: Long expeditions require decisions about growth thresholds and survival
Failure is recoverable but exact travel and combat must be repeated
observationThemes.factualDescription: The original single-player campaign uses health, extra lives, saved levels, and a starting-palace continue state; exhausting lives can forfeit current unassigned experience while preserving major progress.
observationThemes.interpretation: Separating persistent growth from attempt-bound value creates a recoverable failure loop that gives both combat execution and the timing of level choices strategic weight.

contributionHypotheses: A regional map can remain legible while local contacts expand into a different movement model for high-detail action.
NPC assistance becomes a strong progression beat when resolving a social condition produces a new, immediately testable verb.
Persistent levels and attempt-bound experience can create route stakes without deleting campaign completion state.
uniqueOrNonportable: The exact princess premise, palace count and layouts, spell and enemy names, character identities, audiovisual motifs, and final qualification sequence must remain source-specific evidence.
The precise experience tables and loss penalties are balance data, not a reusable pattern; any adaptation requires independent tuning and gentler accessibility review.
openQuestions: A version audit should document exact save and experience-loss differences between the Japanese Disk System original and later cartridge or emulated editions.
